# PsyMetrics

PsyMetrics launched "AI Test Architect" (July 2026), a chat tool that turns an uploaded job description into a role-specific pre-employment assessment. PsyMetrics is pitching this as anchoring generative assessment-building in 30 years of psychometric science and compliance. As AI-generated assessments proliferate, this is the direction of travel: letting non-assessment experts auto-build tests for new roles and filling demand faster. The risk: "compliance-supportive" labeling is not the same as validated fairness; validate that any auto-built test is job-related and defensible before it gates candidates.

## Timeline

- **13 Jul 2026** — PsyMetrics launched "AI Test Architect," a chat tool that turns an uploaded job description into a role-specific pre-employment assessment, pitched as anchoring generative test-building in established psychometric science and compliance. As AI-generated assessments proliferate, this is the direction of travel — but validate that any auto-built test is job-related and defensible before it gates candidates, rather than trusting the "compliance-supportive" label. — [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/07/13/3326119/0/en/The-Antidote-to-Hiring-Bias-PsyMetrics-Launches-AI-Test-Architect-to-Anchor-Generative-Assessment-Building-in-30-Years-of-Psychometric-Science.html)

## Open questions

- How does PsyMetrics' tool handle edge-case job families where valid assessment is contested (e.g., soft-skill-heavy roles, roles newly transformed by AI)?
- What adverse-impact testing does the tool itself run on generated assessments?
